description: When InpUseMTF=true, a two-layer TF confirmation is enforced: HTF (default H1, configurable to H4) scans and caches zone data per symbol using bar-based updates via g_mtf.RefreshHTF(). LTF (default M5, configurable to M1) is used for actual entry scanning. Three MTF gates must pass: (1) HTF zone must be valid (IsHTFReady), (2) current LTF mid-price must be inside the HTF zone with ±10% buffer (IsInHTFZone), (3) LTF signal direction must match HTF direction (DirectionMatch). All MTF failures are silent skips (no log spam). When InpUseMTF=false, single-TF mode uses PERIOD_CURRENT.
source_file: C:\Users\ADMIN\Desktop\12.SMC_System\SMC_Universal_EA_v3_0_fix17\MQL5\Experts\SMC_Universal_EA_v3.0.mq5

topic: SMC Multi-Condition Entry Zone (W/M + BOS + FVG + Zone 50-62% + OB)
description: Entry requires confluence of multiple SMC conditions scanned by CSMCScanner: (1) W/M pattern (double bottom/top) with tolerance InpW23Tol pips and lookback InpW23Lookback bars. (2) BOS (Break of Structure) confirmation. (3) FVG (Fair Value Gap) — up to InpMaxFVG gaps within InpFVGLookback bars. (4) Price must be in 50-62% retracement zone (Fibonacci OTE zone) with InpZoneBase buffer. (5) OB (Order Block) used for SL placement. Three optional gate filters: InpRequireTrend (HH+HL for bull / LH+LL for bear), InpRequireSweep (BSL/SSL liquidity sweep before entry), InpStrictCHoCH (direction-specific CHoCH before FVG). Entry price, SL, 1R, and TP are computed via CSMCScanner::CalcEntry using ATR.

description: CSMCCircuitBreaker monitors cumulative loss percentage. Initialized with InpCBLossPct (default 3.0%). On each new bar, g_cb.Check() is called; if IsTripped() returns true, all new entry logic is skipped for that bar. This acts as a daily/session drawdown kill-switch preventing further entries after a defined loss threshold is breached.

topic: ATR-Based Lot Sizing with Risk Percent
description: Position sizing is handled by CSMCMoneyManager using a fixed risk-per-trade percentage (InpRiskPercent, default 1.0%). Lot size is calculated based on ATR-derived stop loss distance (1R = distance from entry to OB-based SL). ATR is retrieved per symbol via g_sm.GetATR(si); if ATR is zero or unavailable, it falls back to the previous bar's high-low range, or 50 points as a last resort. Spread filter InpMaxSpreadPts (default 50 points) prevents entry during wide spread conditions.

topic: Per-Symbol Magic Number with Cooldown and Position Limits
description: Each symbol in the watchlist receives a unique magic number: magic = InpMagicBase + symbolIndex (e.g., EURUSD at index 0 = 20250303, GBPUSD at index 1 = 20250304). This prevents order conflicts across symbols. Maximum concurrent positions across all symbols is capped by InpMaxPositions (default 10). Per-symbol cooldown of InpCooldownBars (default 3 bars) is enforced after a trade closes — lastTradeBar is updated at CLOSE time for accurate cooldown. SyncOnRestart restores active trade tracking after EA reload.

description: Trailing stop is R-multiple based, not pip-based. Trail activates when profit reaches InpTrailStartR × 1R (default 1.5R). Once active, stop is moved in increments of InpTrailStepR × 1R (default 0.5R). Take profit is set at InpSimTP_R × 1R (default 6.0R). This creates a risk-reward framework where SL, trail trigger, trail step, and TP are all expressed as multiples of the initial risk (1R = entry to initial SL distance). TrailAll() and MonitorAll() are called on every new bar.

description: CSMCNewsFilter blocks entries within InpNewsBlockMin minutes (default 30) before and after high-impact news events for the specific symbol being scanned. The filter is refreshed on every new bar via g_nf.Refresh(). If a symbol is blocked, a SMCEVT_NEWS journal event is logged and the symbol is skipped silently for that bar.

topic: Session Filter — Window Mode with Friday Block

A Friday close block prevents new entries from InpFridayHour onwards on Fridays (InpBlockFriday=true, default hour=20). The entire filter can be toggled via InpTFEnabled. Implemented in CSMCTimeFilter class, checked on every new bar via g_tf.IsAllowed(). Block Mode was explicitly removed in fix16, leaving only Window mode.

topic: Circuit Breaker Trip Gate — IsTripped() Guard Pattern
description: The class exposes a public IsTripped() boolean accessor intended to be polled by the parent EA on every tick or bar before placing any new orders. When tripped, the EA must skip all order placement logic for the rest of the day. This is a hard gate pattern: once m_tripped is true, the Check() method itself short-circuits immediately (early return), preventing redundant loss recalculations and log spam. The trip is only cleared by RecordDay() on the next valid reset cycle.
category: Portfolio Limits / Drawdown Protection / Equity Management
source_file: C:\Users\ADMIN\Desktop\12.SMC_System\SMC_Universal_EA_v3_0_fix17\MQL5\Include\SMC_Universal\SMC_CircuitBreaker.mqh

description: Implements a daily equity drawdown protection mechanism (circuit breaker) that monitors intraday equity loss as a percentage of the day's starting equity. When cumulative loss exceeds the configured threshold (CB_LOSS_PCT), the breaker 'trips' (m_tripped=true) and halts further trading for the remainder of that day. The breaker resets automatically at a configurable hour (CB_RESET_HOUR) on the next calendar day by re-snapshotting the current equity as the new day-start baseline. The trip state is tracked per day-of-year to prevent re-entry on the same day.
category: Portfolio Limits / Drawdown Protection / Equity Management
source_file: C:\Users\ADMIN\Desktop\12.SMC_System\SMC_Universal_EA_v3_0_fix17\MQL5\Include\SMC_Universal\SMC_CircuitBreaker.mqh

description: The DailyLossPct() method provides a live, on-demand calculation of the current intraday equity drawdown as a percentage of the day-start equity snapshot. Formula: (dayStartEquity - currentEquity) / dayStartEquity * 100. This can be used by external modules (e.g., dashboard, risk monitor) to display or act on real-time drawdown without triggering the breaker logic itself. Includes a guard against division by zero when m_dayStartEquity <= 0.
category: Portfolio Limits / Drawdown Protection / Equity Management
source_file: C:\Users\ADMIN\Desktop\12.SMC_System\SMC_Universal_EA_v3_0_fix17\MQL5\Include\SMC_Universal\SMC_CircuitBreaker.mqh

description: On circuit breaker trip, the event is logged to a centralized journal object (CSMCJournal) using a typed event code SMCEVT_CB, instrument tag 'ALL', a formatted message string, and the absolute P&L delta (eq - m_dayStartEquity, which will be negative on loss). This structured logging pattern allows downstream analytics or alerting systems to filter and process circuit breaker events independently from other EA events. The journal pointer is nullable — the class degrades gracefully to Print() only if no journal is injected.
category: Portfolio Limits / Drawdown Protection / Equity Management
code_snippet: if(m_jn!=NULL) m_jn.Log(SMCEVT_CB, "ALL", msg, eq-m_dayStartEquity);
source_file: C:\Users\ADMIN\Desktop\12.SMC_System\SMC_Universal_EA_v3_0_fix17\MQL5\Include\SMC_Universal\SMC_CircuitBreaker.mqh

topic: Daily Equity Snapshot and Auto-Reset at Configurable Hour
description: At the start of each new trading day (detected by comparing DayOfYear() against m_lastResetDay), the circuit breaker resets its trip state and records a fresh equity snapshot via AccountInfoDouble(ACCOUNT_EQUITY). The reset is triggered only when the current server hour equals CB_RESET_HOUR (a compile-time constant from SMC_Defines.mqh), ensuring the baseline is captured at a consistent, predictable time each day (e.g., session open). This prevents stale baselines from carrying over across days.
source_file: C:\Users\ADMIN\Desktop\12.SMC_System\SMC_Universal_EA_v3_0_fix17\MQL5\Include\SMC_Universal\SMC_CircuitBreaker.mqh

topic: SMC Event Journal - In-Memory Ring Buffer with CSV Persistence
description: CSMCJournal implements a dual-layer logging system: a fixed-size in-memory circular buffer (JOURNAL_MAX entries) and a persistent CSV file (JOURNAL_FILE). When the buffer is full, it performs a left-shift eviction (oldest entry dropped, all entries shift left by one index). Each log entry captures: timestamp (TimeCurrent()), event type (ENUM_SMC_EVENT), symbol, description string, and PnL value. The CSV file is opened in FILE_WRITE|FILE_READ|FILE_CSV|FILE_ANSI mode, seeked to EOF before each write, and flushed immediately after each entry (FileFlush) to prevent data loss on crash. Header row is written only if file is empty (FileTell==0 check).
code_snippet: if(m_count >= JOURNAL_MAX) { for(int k=0; k<JOURNAL_MAX-1; k++) m_log[k] = m_log[k+1]; m_count = JOURNAL_MAX-1; }

topic: PnL Aggregation and Event Statistics via Journal
description: The journal provides two analytical methods: TotalPnL() iterates all buffered entries summing pnl fields (useful for session P&L tracking), and CountByEvent(ENUM_SMC_EVENT) counts occurrences of a specific event type (e.g., count circuit breaker triggers, count skipped signals). GetRecent(out[], n) returns the last N entries in reverse-chronological order. These methods enable the EA's risk management layer to query journal state for adaptive decision-making (e.g., if CountByEvent(SMCEVT_CB) > threshold, increase caution).
code_snippet:
double TotalPnL() { double s=0; for(int i=0;i<m_count;i++) s+=m_log[i].pnl; return s; }
int CountByEvent(ENUM_SMC_EVENT evt) { int c=0; for(int i=0;i<m_count;i++) if(m_log[i].eventType==evt) c++; return c; }
source_file: C:\Users\ADMIN\Desktop\12.SMC_System\SMC_Universal_EA_v3_0_fix17\MQL5\Include\SMC_Universal\SMC_Journal.mqh

topic: Session and News Filter Event Tracking

This confirms the EA enforces time-based trading windows and news blackout periods. The journal records these filter activations with symbol and description context, allowing analysis of how often trades are blocked by time/news filters versus actually executed.

topic: HTF Directional Bias Alignment (Trend Confluence Filter)
description: DirectionMatch() enforces that the LTF trade direction (bull/bear) must match the HTF zone's isBull flag. If the HTF zone is bearish but the LTF signal is bullish, the trade is blocked. When MTF is disabled (m_enabled=false), both IsInHTFZone() and DirectionMatch() return true, acting as a bypass — allowing the EA to operate as a pure LTF system without HTF filtering. This is a critical confluence gate in the entry logic pipeline.
source_file: C:\Users\ADMIN\Desktop\12.SMC_System\SMC_Universal_EA_v3_0_fix17\MQL5\Include\SMC_Universal\SMC_MTFManager.mqh

topic: HTF Zone Proximity Check with Buffer and FVG Overlay
description: The IsInHTFZone() method uses a three-tier price proximity check: (1) Price within the HTF zone boundaries extended by ±10% of zone width as a buffer (zWidth * 0.1), allowing slight overshoots; (2) Price within a Fair Value Gap (FVG) sub-zone defined by fvgHi/fvgLo inside the HTF zone; (3) Either condition satisfies the filter. This means entries can trigger slightly outside the raw zone if within the buffer, or precisely within an FVG imbalance area. The zone is defined by zHi/zLo from a ZoneResult struct (likely Order Block or Supply/Demand zone from SMC methodology).
source_file: C:\Users\ADMIN\Desktop\12.SMC_System\SMC_Universal_EA_v3_0_fix17\MQL5\Include\SMC_Universal\SMC_MTFManager.mqh

topic: Multi-Timeframe (MTF) Zone Filtering — HTF Cache System
description: The EA implements a two-layer MTF confirmation system. HTF (H1/H4) zones are scanned via CSMCScanner and cached per symbol in an HTFCache struct. The cache is only refreshed when a new HTF bar opens (detected by comparing iTime(sym, m_htfTF, 0) against the stored barTime), conserving CPU resources. LTF (M5/M1) entries are only permitted when: (1) the HTF cache is valid/ready, (2) the current price falls within the HTF zone, and (3) the LTF directional bias matches the HTF zone direction. This prevents counter-trend LTF entries and filters noise.
source_file: C:\Users\ADMIN\Desktop\12.SMC_System\SMC_Universal_EA_v3_0_fix17\MQL5\Include\SMC_Universal\SMC_MTFManager.mqh

topic: Multi-Symbol Portfolio Architecture with Index-Based Cache
description: The MTF manager is designed for multi-symbol scanning. CSymbolManager provides the symbol list (Count() / GetSymbol(i)), and all per-symbol data (HTFCache[], ATR handles[]) are stored in parallel arrays indexed by symbol index (si). Array bounds are checked on every access (si<0||si>=ArraySize(m_cache)) to prevent out-of-bounds crashes. This architecture supports running the EA across a portfolio of instruments simultaneously with independent HTF zone states per symbol.
category: Portfolio Limits / Drawdown Protection / Equity Management

topic: Per-Symbol ATR Handle Management on HTF
description: Each symbol gets its own ATR(14) indicator handle on the HTF timeframe, initialized during Init() and stored in m_hATR[]. The ATR value is retrieved via CopyBuffer() from bar index 1 (last closed bar) to avoid repainting. Handles are properly released in Deinit() via IndicatorRelease() to prevent memory/handle leaks. The HTF ATR is available for downstream use in LTF entry calculations (e.g., SL/TP sizing relative to HTF volatility).

topic: Dependency on CSymbolManager for Lot Normalization
description: The money manager class does not independently normalize lots — it delegates to CSymbolManager.NormLot(sym, rawLot). This means lot normalization logic (SYMBOL_VOLUME_MIN, SYMBOL_VOLUME_MAX, SYMBOL_VOLUME_STEP rounding) is centralized in CSymbolManager. A null pointer guard is implemented: if m_sm==NULL, CalcLot() returns 0.0, preventing invalid order submissions. SetSymbolManager() must be called during EA initialization before any lot calculation is attempted.

topic: Input Validation Guards in Lot Calculation
description: CalcLot() implements three defensive guards before computing: (1) SL distance must be > 0 to prevent division by zero, (2) m_sm pointer must not be NULL, (3) both TickValue and TickSize from broker must be > 0. If any condition fails, the method returns 0.0 as a safe fallback. This prevents NaN/infinity results from being passed to order functions. The SetRiskPct() setter also enforces positivity: if pct <= 0, it falls back to RISK_PCT_DEF.
source_file: C:\Users\ADMIN\Desktop\12.SMC_System\SMC_Universal_EA_v3_0_fix17\MQL5\Include\SMC_Universal\SMC_MoneyManager.mqh

description: Risk is calculated as a fixed percentage of ACCOUNT_BALANCE (not equity). The default percentage is sourced from the constant RISK_PCT_DEF defined in SMC_Defines.mqh. This means floating unrealized P&L does not inflate or deflate the risk amount per trade — only realized balance is used. The risk amount in currency units is: RiskAmount = Balance × RiskPct / 100. This is a conservative approach that prevents compounding losses during drawdown periods since equity-based sizing would reduce lot size automatically during open losing trades.
code_snippet:
double RiskAmount() const { return GetBalance()*m_riskPct/100.0; }
// GetBalance() uses ACCOUNT_BALANCE, not ACCOUNT_EQUITY
source_file: C:\Users\ADMIN\Desktop\12.SMC_System\SMC_Universal_EA_v3_0_fix17\MQL5\Include\SMC_Universal\SMC_MoneyManager.mqh

description: The CalcRisk() method provides a reverse calculation — given an existing lot size, entry price, and SL price, it returns the exact monetary risk exposure in account currency. This is used for validation, logging, or pre-trade risk checks. Formula: Risk = Lot × (SL_distance / TickSize) × TickValue. This mirrors the inverse of CalcLot() and can be used to verify that a normalized lot (after broker rounding) still falls within acceptable risk tolerance before order submission.
source_file: C:\Users\ADMIN\Desktop\12.SMC_System\SMC_Universal_EA_v3_0_fix17\MQL5\Include\SMC_Universal\SMC_MoneyManager.mqh

topic: Risk-Based Lot Sizing via SL Distance (Universal Asset Formula)
description: The core lot sizing engine uses a universal formula applicable to any asset class (Forex, Indices, Commodities, Crypto). It calculates the required lot size by dividing the risk amount (in account currency) by the monetary value of the SL distance. The formula normalizes the SL distance into tick units first: Lot = RiskAmount / ((SL_distance / TickSize) × TickValue). This correctly handles instruments with non-standard tick sizes (e.g., XAUUSD with tickSize=0.01, US30 with tickSize=1.0) by converting price distance into a tick count before multiplying by per-tick monetary value. The result is then normalized via CSymbolManager.NormLot() to conform to broker lot step and min/max constraints.
code_snippet:
double rawLot = riskAmt / (dist/tickSize*tickVal);
return m_sm.NormLot(sym, rawLot);
source_file: C:\Users\ADMIN\Desktop\12.SMC_System\SMC_Universal_EA_v3_0_fix17\MQL5\Include\SMC_Universal\SMC_MoneyManager.mqh

description: The blocking window around a news event is configurable via SetBlockMin(int min), defaulting to 30 minutes. The block applies symmetrically: any high-impact event within ±blockMin minutes of the current server time (TimeCurrent()) will cause IsBlocked() to return true, preventing new order placement. The window is applied as an absolute time difference (MathAbs), meaning it blocks both before and after the event.

topic: Currency Pair Decomposition for News Matching
description: The filter extracts the base currency (first 3 chars) and quote currency (next 3 chars) from the symbol name using StringSubstr(). It then checks if any cached high-impact event's country currency matches either the base or quote currency of the traded pair. This approach assumes standard 6-character Forex symbol naming and may not handle suffixed symbols (e.g., EURUSDm, EURUSD.pro) correctly without additional normalization.

description: If CalendarValueHistory() returns a negative value (API failure), m_calAvail is set to false and IsBlocked() always returns false, effectively disabling the news filter without crashing the EA. A one-time warning is printed via Print() using the m_warnedUnavail flag to prevent log spam. This is a defensive programming pattern ensuring the EA continues operating even when the broker's calendar feed is unavailable.

topic: High-Impact News Filter via MT5 Calendar API
description: Implements a news filter using MT5's native CalendarValueHistory() API to fetch high-impact economic events. Only events with CALENDAR_IMPORTANCE_HIGH are stored. The filter checks if the current symbol's base or quote currency has a high-impact news event within a configurable time window (default ±30 minutes). If the MT5 calendar is unavailable (e.g., no internet or broker restriction), the filter is silently disabled after a single warning print, allowing trading to continue unblocked.

topic: News Event Cache with 24-Hour Rolling Window
description: To avoid repeated expensive CalendarValueHistory() API calls on every tick, the filter caches up to 200 high-impact events (SMC_NEWS_MAX=200) covering a 24-hour forward window (SMC_NEWS_CACHE_H=24). The cache is only refreshed when the current time falls outside the previously cached [m_cacheFrom, m_cacheTo) range. This is a performance optimization pattern critical for tick-based EAs.

description: ParseSymbols() tokenizes a raw input string using comma, semicolon, or space as delimiters. Each token is trimmed of whitespace and converted to uppercase via StringToUpper(). Empty tokens are skipped. Output array is pre-allocated to MAX_SYMBOLS then resized to actual count. This allows flexible user input like 'EURUSD,GBPUSD, XAUUSD' or 'eurusd;gbpusd xauusd'.

description: ResolveSuffix() scans ALL broker symbols (SymbolsTotal(false) — including hidden) to match a base symbol name like 'XAUUSD' to its broker-specific variant like 'XAUUSD.iux'. Matching rules: symbol must start with baseName, and the next character (if any) must be '.', lowercase letter, digit, '+', or '-'. Among all matches, the shortest symbol name is selected (minimum suffix). Falls back to Market Watch scan if full scan fails. This enables the EA to work across brokers without manual suffix configuration.

GetATR() reads bar[1] (closed bar) via CopyBuffer to avoid repainting. PipValuePerLot() computes account-currency pip value using SYMBOL_TRADE_TICK_VALUE / SYMBOL_TRADE_TICK_SIZE * PipSize(). These two values together enable ATR-proportional position sizing: lot = RiskAmount / (ATR_pips * PipValuePerLot).

description: NormLot() enforces broker volume constraints by flooring raw lot size to the nearest SYMBOL_VOLUME_STEP increment (using MathFloor(raw/step)*step), then clamping to [SYMBOL_VOLUME_MIN, SYMBOL_VOLUME_MAX]. Default step fallback is 0.01 if broker returns 0. Result is NormalizeDouble'd to 2 decimal places. This prevents order rejection due to invalid lot sizes.

topic: IsAllowed() Gate Function — Unified Entry Permission Check
description: Single public method IsAllowed() acts as the master gate for time-based trade permission. Returns true only when: filter is disabled OR (not weekend AND not Friday-close AND inside at least one active session window). Designed to be called at the start of OnTick() or order placement logic to short-circuit all entry logic when outside allowed windows.

description: Implements a dual-session time filter using HHMM integer format. Supports two configurable trading windows (Session 1 and Session 2). If the filter is disabled (m_enabled=false), all times are permitted. Session 1 defaults to 14:00–15:00 and Session 2 defaults to 20:30–21:00 server time.

topic: Weekend Block — Hard Coded Saturday/Sunday Exclusion
description: Unconditionally blocks all trading on Saturday (dow==6) and Sunday (dow==0) regardless of any other configuration. This check is performed before session window checks and cannot be overridden by the Configure() method. Uses MqlDateTime.day_of_week where 0=Sunday, 6=Saturday.

if(dow==0 || dow==6) return false; // Always block weekend

topic: Hybrid Grid Entry with 5 Trade Modes
description: Five ENUM_TRADE_MODE options control grid order placement direction and lot progression: T_FOLLOW_FIXED (fixed lot, trend-following only), T_FOLLOW_STEP (increasing lot, trend-following), T_AGAINST_STEP (increasing lot, counter-trend/martingale), T_BOTH_STEP (increasing lot, both directions), T_HYBRID_STEP (fixed lot trend-following + increasing lot counter-trend). In HYBRID mode: isF (price moved gridDist in trade direction) → add initLot; isA (price moved gridDist against) → add lastLot + initLot. 5-second cooldown per side prevents duplicate orders during execution lag.

Three resume modes: RESUME_HOUR (after N hours), RESUME_MARKET_OPEN (at specific hour next day, min 1h gap), RESUME_MANUAL (no auto-resume). Recovery factor resets to 1.0 when equity > balance.

description: Grid spacing is calculated from ATR Week(4) / ATR Day(14) ratio multiplied by InpMinGridPoints, with per-order expansion via InpGridStep (+10% per order index). description: Entry signals are gated by a custom market regime classifier using two metrics: SC100 (sign-change ratio over 100 M1 bars — measures mean-reversion tendency) and Beta1 (OLS autocorrelation of 50 M1 returns — measures momentum persistence). Four regimes: CRASH (SC100<0.22, Beta1>0.30, volume spike >5× avg), TRENDING (SC100<0.25), REVERTING (SC100>0.35), WEAK (default). REVERTING regime uses RSI<40/RSI>60 + SMA filter + PDH/PDL or round-number S/R zone proximity (ATR×1.5). TRENDING regime uses EMA9/EMA50 crossover + Beta1 directional confirmation + price within ATR×1.5 of EMA9. CRASH and WEAK regimes block all new entries.

topic: Multi-Layer Drawdown and Daily Circuit Breaker
description: Four independent equity protection layers: (1) Emergency MaxDD%: if real-time DD% (balance vs equity) exceeds InpMaxDD (default 20%), CloseAll() fires immediately. (2) CutLoss per cycle: if open PnL < -InpCutLossR × OneR (default -5R), all positions close and a 4-hour cooldown activates with lot reduced to 50% of initLot on resume. (3) Daily Loss Circuit: if daily PnL < -InpDailyLossR × OneR (default -10R), g_dailyCircuit=true blocks all new entries for the day. (4) Daily Profit Target: if daily PnL > +InpDailyTargetR × OneR (default +15R), g_dailyTarget=true also blocks new entries. OneR = balance × InpRiskPct (0.2% default).

topic: Snowball Grid Recovery with Runner BE Logic

ATR-based TP = SafeATR() × 0.5 × leg_mult. Proximity guard (IsNoNearby) prevents stacking within ATR-ratio distance. Exit: RSI cross triggers exit after minimum 5-bar hold. If total PnL is positive, all positions except the last (newest) are closed, and the last becomes a 'Runner' with SL moved to break-even. If PnL is negative, CloseAll() fires. topic: Initial Mode Determination via ATR Comparison
description: On first run, compares current ATR to average ATR over the period. If current ATR < average, starts in UP mode (lower volatility, expect uptrend). If current ATR > average, starts in DN mode (higher volatility, expect downtrend).
code_snippet: if(iATR(NULL, 0, ATR, limit) < md / limit) { Up[limit+1] = Low[limit+1] - REZ; MODE = "UP"; } if(iATR(NULL, 0, ATR, limit) > md / limit) { Dn[limit+1] = High[limit+1] + REZ; MODE = "DN"; }
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\! JOBOT INVEST Files\Indicators\NRTR_ATR_STOP.mq4

description: Maintains two trading modes (UP/DN) that switch based on price breaking through stop levels. In UP mode, tracks lower stop (Up buffer). In DN mode, tracks upper stop (Dn buffer). Mode flips when price crosses the opposite level.
code_snippet: if(MODE == "DN" &&  Low[i+1] > Dn[i+1]) { Up[i+1] = Low[i+1] - REZ; MODE = "UP"; } if(MODE == "UP" && High[i+1] < Up[i+1]) { Dn[i+1] = High[i+1] + REZ; MODE = "DN"; }
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\! JOBOT INVEST Files\Indicators\NRTR_ATR_STOP.mq4

description: Calculates upper (Dn) and lower (Up) stop levels using ATR (Average True Range) multiplied by a coefficient. The stop distance = Coefficient * ATR(period). This creates adaptive stops that expand/contract with market volatility.
code_snippet: REZ = Coeficient*iATR(NULL, 0, ATR, i); Up[limit+1] = Low[limit+1] - REZ; Dn[limit+1] = High[limit+1] + REZ;
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\! JOBOT INVEST Files\Indicators\NRTR_ATR_STOP.mq4

description: Exposes two external parameters: ATR (lookback period, default 20) and Coefficient (stop distance multiplier, default 2). Allows traders to adjust stop sensitivity without code modification.

description: Stops trail upward in UP mode when Low exceeds previous stop + REZ, or maintain previous level. Similarly, stops trail downward in DN mode when High falls below previous stop - REZ. Creates a ratcheting effect that follows price momentum.

description: Finds one extremum point (left), then searches for the bar with minimal slope (delta) between current bar and extremum. Optimizes trend line angle by minimizing price change rate.
code_snippet: case EXM_DELTA: leftIndex = rates_total - InpLeftExmSide - 2; for ( ; !isLowestLow(leftIndex, InpLeftExmSide, low) && leftIndex > minRequiredBars; leftIndex-- ); curLeftSup.setPoint(low[leftIndex], time[leftIndex]); rightIndex = rates_total - InpFromCurrent - 2; delta = (low[rightIndex] - low[leftIndex]) / (rightIndex - leftIndex); for ( int tmpIndex = rightIndex - 1; tmpIndex > leftIndex; tmpIndex-- ) { tmpDelta = (low[tmpIndex] - curLeftSup.getPrice()) / (tmpIndex - leftIndex); if ( tmpDelta < delta ) { delta = tmpDelta; rightIndex = tmpIndex; } }

description: The EA draws multiple chart objects: breakout lines (dashed, colored by direction) at pivot levels, signal labels (CHoCH/BOS text), entry markers (up/down arrows), and TP lines (cyan, extending right). description: Optional dual-EMA trend filter using fast (default 50) and slow (default 200) EMAs. Can be toggled on/off. Alternative single-EMA mode compares price directly to EMA. This filter can be used to confirm trend direction before taking breakout signals, though the code shows signal generation without explicit EMA validation in CheckForSignals().

topic: New Bar Detection and Signal Processing
description: The EA uses iTime() to detect new bars by comparing current bar time to lastBarTime. On new bars, UpdatePivots() recalculates swing highs/lows. Signal checking (CheckForSignals) only occurs when no trade is active. During active trades, CheckTradeManagement() monitors position status and UpdateTPLine() refreshes the TP visualization. This prevents multiple signals on the same bar and ensures clean trade flow.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\AI GEN\Smart Money Breakouts EA.mq5

description: The EA identifies swing highs and lows using a configurable period (default 20 bars). It searches for pivot points by comparing each bar's high/low against 'period' bars on both sides. When price breaks above a pivot high (CHoCH/BOS signal), a bullish breakout is triggered; when price breaks below a pivot low, a bearish breakout is triggered. The pivot state is tracked with phActive and plActive flags, and hBreak flag tracks whether a high breakout has occurred to distinguish between CHoCH (Change of Character) and BOS (Break of Structure) signals.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\AI GEN\Smart Money Breakouts EA.mq5

description: The EA calculates green volume (close > open) and red volume (close < open) percentages over a lookback period. A threshold (default 55%) determines signal quality: Green signal when green volume exceeds threshold, Red signal when red volume exceeds threshold, Yellow when neither dominates. The filter can skip trades with yellow volume (indecision) or bad volume direction (red volume on long entries, green volume on short entries). This prevents trading against volume confirmation.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\AI GEN\Smart Money Breakouts EA.mq5

topic: One Trade at a Time Portfolio Limit
description: The InpOneTradeAtTime parameter (default true) enforces a maximum of one active position. When a trade is active (tradeActive flag), new entry signals are rejected with a 'Trade already active' message. This prevents over-leveraging and ensures focus on single trade management. The trade is marked inactive only after position is closed and detected via PositionSelect() failure.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\AI GEN\Smart Money Breakouts EA.mq5

topic: ATR-Based Stop Loss and Risk-Reward Sizing
description: Stop loss is calculated as current close price minus (ATR × multiplier) for longs, and current close price plus (ATR × multiplier) for shorts. The multiplier defaults to 1.5. Take profit is then set using the risk-reward ratio: TP = Entry ± (ATR × Multiplier × RR Ratio). This ensures consistent risk sizing relative to volatility. The ATR period is configurable (default 14), and all prices are normalized to the symbol's digit precision.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\AI GEN\Smart Money Breakouts EA.mq5

topic: Trade Closure Detection and Win/Loss Tracking
description: The EA monitors active positions via PositionSelect(). When a position closes, it searches the deal history (last 60 seconds) for the most recent closed trade matching the magic number and symbol. If profit > 0, winCount increments; otherwise lossCount increments. The TP line color changes to lime green (solid) for wins or orange-red (dotted) for losses. Win rate is calculated as winCount / (winCount + lossCount) × 100 and displayed on dashboard with color coding (green ≥60%, yellow ≥50%, orange <50%).
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\AI GEN\Smart Money Breakouts EA.mq5

description: Uses ATR indicator (14-period default) to identify positions too close to current price. Nearby distance = ATR * multiplier (1.5x default). Separately checks buy positions nearby (within ATR distance of ASK) and sell positions nearby (within ATR distance of BID). Can prevent new entries if nearby position exists. Tracks nearby positions in arrays with ticket, type, open_price, distance, lots, and comment.

description: The EA tracks maximum profit reached and implements two profit-taking modes: absolute dollar amount or percentage-based. Time-based filtering (Time1, Time2) restricts checks to specific time windows. This prevents grid clustering and reduces correlation risk.

description: Global variables (v::RSI_B, v::RSI_S, v::LOTB, v::LOTS, v::ATR_Near) computed via formula blocks using arithmetic operators (+, -, *, /). RSI values adjusted by subtracting 50 baseline. Pre-SL pips are calculated before order placement to determine lot size.

description: The EA implements multiple lot sizing modes including fixed lots, percentage-based (equity/balance/freemargin), risk-based (equityRisk/balanceRisk/freemarginRisk with stop loss distance), and advanced methods like Fixed Ratio, Martingale, Fibonacci, Dalembert, Labouchere, and 1326 betting systems. Lot sizing is controlled via VolumeMode parameter with risk calculated as: lots = DynamicLots(Symbol, VolumeMode, VolumeRisk, pre_sl_pips) where pre_sl_pips is derived from stop loss distance. topic: Lot Size Upper Limit and Alignment
description: The AlignLots() function enforces broker-specific constraints: minimum lot size (SYMBOL_VOLUME_MIN), maximum lot size (SYMBOL_VOLUME_MAX), lot step (SYMBOL_VOLUME_STEP), and optional upper/lower limits. VolumeUpperLimit parameter in trading blocks allows user-defined caps. All calculated lots are rounded to the nearest lot step and clamped within valid ranges before order submission.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\EA Week3\(Jobot) Risk Percent of Equity Lot EA.mq5

topic: Risk Percent of Equity Lot Sizing
description: The EA implements dynamic lot sizing based on a configurable Risk parameter (default 10.0). In Block0 and Block5, VolumeMode is set to 'equityRisk' which calculates lot size using DynamicLots() function with VolumeRisk parameter. The risk percentage is passed from input parameter Risk to the trading blocks via c::Risk variable, allowing position sizing to scale with account equity changes.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\EA Week3\(Jobot) Risk Percent of Equity Lot EA.mq5

topic: Virtual Stops and Emergency Hard Stops
description: The EA supports virtual stops (VIRTUAL_STOPS_ENABLED) which are managed client-side via VirtualStopsDriver() called on every tick. When virtual stops are active, the system can optionally use emergency hard stops (EMERGENCY_STOPS_REL parameter) as a safety net. Virtual stops are disabled during optimization and non-visual backtesting. AlignStopLoss() and AlignTakeProfit() functions check broker freeze levels and minimum stop distances, with virtual stops allowing tighter levels than broker minimums.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\EA Week3\(Jobot) Risk Percent of Equity Lot EA.mq5

description: The EA uses a block-based system where each trading logic component (entry signals, filters, order placement) is a separate block class inheriting from BlockCalls. Blocks have inbound/outbound connections forming a directed graph. Block execution is controlled via callbacks: when a block condition is met, it calls _callback_() which triggers connected blocks. This allows flexible chaining of conditions (e.g., MACD divergence → Once per bar → Buy order).
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\EA Week3\(Jobot) Risk Percent of FreeMargin Lot EA.mq4

topic: Dynamic Stop Loss and Take Profit Modes
description: The EA supports multiple stop loss and take profit calculation modes: fixed pips, dynamic pips, dynamic digits, dynamic level (from lowest/highest price over candle period), percent of price, and percent relationships (SL as % of TP or TP as % of SL). Stop loss can be set as a price level (sll) or pips distance (slp). The AlignStopLoss() and AlignTakeProfit() functions validate stops against minimum distance requirements and freeze levels, with virtual stops option for broker restrictions.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\EA Week3\(Jobot) Risk Percent of FreeMargin Lot EA.mq4

topic: MACD Divergence Detection for Entry Signals
description: Two blocks implement MACD divergence detection: MDL_macdDivBull detects bullish divergences (lower MACD lows with higher price lows), and MDL_macdDivBear detects bearish divergences (higher MACD highs with lower price highs). Both use configurable fastEMA (12), slowEMA (26), signalSMA (9), and macdThreshold (0.0003). Divergences are drawn as trend lines on chart. These trigger buy/sell orders through block callbacks.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\EA Week3\(Jobot) Risk Percent of FreeMargin Lot EA.mq4

description: MDL_OncePerBar model ensures trading logic executes maximum once per bar (or configurable PassMaxTimes). Uses static token-based tracking combining symbol and timeframe to maintain separate state for each symbol/timeframe combination. Prevents multiple entries on same bar by comparing bar time (iTime) and only allowing execution when new bar opens. Essential for preventing over-trading on fast ticks.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\EA Week3\(Jobot) Risk Percent of FreeMargin Lot EA.mq4

topic: Trade Group Tracking for Multi-Strategy Management
description: The Group parameter (default empty string) allows grouping trades for separate money management. Recovery functions (Martingale, Fibonacci, etc.) track trade history by group using TradeSelectByIndex() and HistoryTradeSelectByIndex() with group filtering. This enables running multiple independent trading strategies on same symbol with separate lot progression sequences. MagicStart parameter provides base magic number with group offset.
category: Portfolio Limits / Drawdown Protection / Equity Management

BetLabouchere uses the Labouchere cancellation system with configurable number lists. BetDalembert adds/subtracts initial lots. Bet1326 follows the 1-3-2-6 progression. Key variables calculated: v::Nearby (grid spacing), v::TP (take profit), v::Lotx, v::Loty, v::Lotz (lot sizes), v::First_Order_Price, v::Half_Order_Price. Formulas validate EMPTY_VALUE to prevent invalid calculations.

topic: Grid Entry with No Nearby Trade Detection
description: Block 0 (MDL_NoNearbyRunning) checks if there are no open trades within a specified range (RangeFraction or RangePips) from the current price before placing new orders. This prevents clustering of orders too close together. The range can be positioned at center (case 0), above (case 1), or below (case 2) the existing order price.

description: Block 4 (MDL_eTrade_TradeNew) triggers on new trade creation. Block 13 (MDL_LoopStartTrades) iterates through open trades with multiple sort options: newest-to-oldest, oldest-to-newest, profitable-first, profitable-last. Block 17 (MDL_CheckUProfit) monitors unrealized profit with both money and pips modes, supporting individual trade filters (EachProfitMode). Block 16 closes trades after OrderMinutes have elapsed.

description: AccountBalanceAtStart() captures initial balance on first run (static memory). The EA tracks account balance via AccountInfoDouble(ACCOUNT_BALANCE). topic: Grid Recovery Logic with Martingale Betting
description: The EA implements multiple recovery strategies including Martingale (BetMartingale), 1326 betting (Bet1326), Fibonacci (BetFibonacci), Dalembert (BetDalembert), and Labouchere (BetLabouchere) systems. These are triggered after trade events to scale lot sizes based on profit/loss outcomes. The Martingale multiplier increases lots on loss (mmMgMultiplyOnLoss) and can reset on profit (mmMgResetOnProfit). The 1326 system uses a sequence: after profit, multiply by 3, then 6, then 2, then reset to initial.

topic: Dynamic Lot Sizing with Risk Management

Risk-based modes calculate lots using pre_sl_pips (stop loss in pips). Lots are aligned using AlignLots() which respects SYMBOL_VOLUME_STEP, SYMBOL_VOLUME_MIN, and SYMBOL_VOLUME_MAX constraints. topic: Conditional Order Placement with Trade Proximity Detection
description: Block 0 (MDL_NoNearbyRunning) prevents order clustering by checking if any existing trade is within a specified range of the current price. It supports three range position modes: centered (distance/2 on both sides), above open price, or below open price. The model filters trades by time window (Time1 to Time2) and checks distance in either pips or fractions. Only when no nearby trade exists does it trigger Block 1 to place a new order, implementing a safety mechanism against over-trading in tight ranges.

description: The EA uses 8 formula blocks (Block 2, 3, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 15) to calculate intermediate values through mathematical operations. Block 2 calculates v::Nearby by dividing v::First_Order_Price by c::Bullet and multiplying by 2. Block 3 calculates v::TP (take profit) similarly. Blocks 7-11 calculate lot sizes (v::Lotx, v::Loty, v::Lotz) and price levels (v::Half_Order_Price) using division operations. These formulas support operators like '+', '-', '*', '/' and allow dynamic calculation of grid spacing and position sizing based on account balance and trade parameters.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\EA Week5\(Jobot) KZM Grid EA v.3.mq4

description: Block 4 (MDL_eTrade_TradeNew) monitors for new trade creation events and triggers Block 5-6 chain when a new buy order is opened. Block 5 checks if exactly 1 buy trade exists (CompareCount == 1), and Block 6 captures the open price of that first trade into v::First_Order_Price. This price becomes the reference point for calculating subsequent grid levels. The system uses e_Reason() == 'new' to detect only new trades, not modifications, ensuring clean grid initialization.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\EA Week5\(Jobot) KZM Grid EA v.3.mq4

topic: Grid Recovery Logic with Multi-Level Entry
description: The EA implements a grid-based recovery system where it places multiple buy orders at different price levels. Block 0 checks for 'no trade nearby' within a configurable range (using RangeFraction based on v::Nearby variable), preventing orders from clustering. When conditions are met, Block 1 executes a buy order. The system tracks the first order price (v::First_Order_Price) and uses it to calculate subsequent entry levels. Block 13-21 implements a loop-based modification system that adjusts stops and targets on existing positions, with profitable-first and profitable-last sorting to manage grid positions strategically.

topic: Profit-Based Trade Sorting and Selective Modification
description: Blocks 13 and 18 use MDL_LoopStartTrades with LoopDirection set to 'profitable-last' and 'profitable-first' respectively. This sorts trades by profit before iteration, allowing the EA to handle profitable and losing positions differently. Block 13 processes the least profitable trade first (LoopLimit=1), while Block 18 processes the most profitable trade first. Block 20 skips the first profitable trade (LoopSkip=1) to modify only secondary positions. This selective approach enables sophisticated grid management where different positions receive different stop/target adjustments.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\EA Week5\(Jobot) KZM Grid EA v.3.mq4

topic: Unrealized Profit Monitoring with Conditional Trade Closure
description: Block 17 (MDL_CheckUProfit) monitors unrealized profit across buy positions with multiple profit calculation modes: money-based, pips-based, or pips-sum. It calculates average entry price and current profit in both currency and pips. When profit exceeds ProfitAmount (10.0 currency units), it triggers Block 16 to close trades. Block 16 (MDL_CloseOpened) closes all qualifying buy trades that have been open for at least OrderMinutes (0 = immediate). This creates a profit-taking mechanism that closes the grid when target profit is achieved, with time-based filtering to avoid premature closure.

topic: Block-Based Modular Architecture with Dependency Management
description: The EA uses a 22-block system where each block is a class inheriting from BlockCalls with explicit outbound/inbound block connections. Blocks are initialized in OnInit() with disabled_blocks_list (blocks 4,5,6,8 initially disabled) and blocks_to_run arrays controlling execution flow. The __announceThisBlock() method builds dependency graphs, and the run() method respects FXD_BREAK flags for flow control. OnTick() runs blocks {2,13,17,18,20}, EventTrade() runs block {4}, and OnInit() runs block {12}. This modular design enables complex trading logic through sequential block execution with callback-based inter-block communication.

topic: Profitable Trade Prioritization and Loop Control
description: The EA implements sophisticated trade loop logic with sorting capabilities. Block 13 loops through buy trades in 'profitable-last' order (least profitable first), limiting to 1 trade per iteration. Block 18 uses 'profitable-first' order to process most profitable trades. Block 20 skips the first profitable trade (LoopSkip=1) to process secondary positions. The MDL_LoopStartTrades template supports break/continue simulation (FXD_BREAK, FXD_CONTINUE flags), loop direction control, skip/every/limit parameters, and array-based sorting by profit for complex trade management workflows.

description: The EA generates buy and sell signals by combining specific ZigZag patterns with Relative Strength Index (RSI) conditions. description: The EA's entry logic is based on a combination of four Simple Moving Averages (MA1, MA2, MA3, MA4) and Bollinger Bands. A boolean input 'Band_Trend' determines whether the EA follows a trend-following or counter-trend approach. For buys, it checks if MA1 > MA2 and MA3 > MA4, combined with price being below the Bollinger Upper Band (trend-following) or above the Bollinger Lower Band (counter-trend). For sells, it checks if MA1 < MA2 and MA3 < MA4, combined with price being above the Bollinger Lower Band (trend-following) or below the Bollinger Upper Band (counter-trend). Trades are only placed if no other positions exist for the current symbol.

Specific thresholds for `macd_v` (e.g., >150, >50, >-50, <-50, <-150) determine the assigned market state.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\ICEE PROFIT\template.mq5

description: The presence of an `OCODriver()` function indicates that the EA supports One-Cancels-Other order types. This feature is used to manage linked orders where the execution of one order automatically cancels other pending orders, commonly employed in breakout strategies or for hedging.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\ICEE PROFIT\template.mq5

description: The EA includes logic to disable virtual stops during optimization runs (`MQLInfoInteger(MQL_OPTIMIZATION)`). description: The EA incorporates a virtual stop-loss and take-profit mechanism, controlled by `VIRTUAL_STOPS_ENABLED`, `VIRTUAL_STOPS_TIMEOUT`, `USE_EMERGENCY_STOPS`, `EMERGENCY_STOPS_REL`, and `EMERGENCY_STOPS_ADD` constants. source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\ICEE PROFIT\template.mq5

description: Entry signals are generated based on the detection of three consecutive bull or bear candles. Each of these candles must have a minimum body size (1.0 pips). The EA ensures that this entry logic runs only once per bar and only if there are no existing open positions or pending orders on the symbol.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\ICEE PROFIT\unnamed (1).mq5

description: The EA places pending orders (Buy/Sell Limit/Stop) with entry prices determined by a 'liquidity zone'. This zone is calculated as half the sum of the body sizes of the last three candles. The pending order's price is set at a 'PriceOffset' from the current market price, where the offset value is derived from this calculated liquidity zone. Orders are only placed if the combined candle body size meets a minimum threshold (Liquidity_zones_pip).
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\ICEE PROFIT\unnamed (1).mq5

description: Pending orders are configured with an expiration time of 1 day, automatically skipping weekends. The range can be defined in pips or as a fraction, and the position relative to the existing trade's open price can be specified (e.g., within the range, above, or below). This helps avoid over-exposure in a tight price area or prevents opening trades too close to existing ones.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\TriAngle Runtrend 1 shot V1.01.mq4

description: The EA implements a Martingale lot sizing strategy. It allows for an initial lot size (`mmMgInitialLots`), a multiplier on loss (`mmMgMultiplyOnLoss`), a multiplier on profit (`mmMgMultiplyOnProfit`), and options to add fixed lots on loss (`mmMgAddLotsOnLoss`) or profit (`mmMgAddLotsOnProfit`). It also includes parameters to reset the Martingale sequence on loss (`mmMgResetOnLoss`) or profit (`mmMgResetOnProfit`). This suggests a strategy that increases trade size after losses (classic Martingale) or potentially after profits (Anti-Martingale) to accelerate gains or recover losses.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\TriAngle Runtrend 1 shot V1.01.mq4

description: The EA supports various dynamic lot sizing methods beyond Martingale, including fixed lot size, percentage of equity/balance/freemargin, risk-based sizing (percentage of equity/balance/freemargin risked per trade, or fixed risk amount), fixed ratio, and other betting systems like 1-3-2-6, Fibonacci, D'Alembert, and Labouchere. This provides a comprehensive suite of money management options.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\TriAngle Runtrend 1 shot V1.01.mq4

topic: Flexible Stop Loss and Take Profit Calculation
description: Stop Loss and Take Profit levels can be defined in multiple ways: fixed pips, dynamic pips (from a function), dynamic digits (from a function), dynamic price level (from a function), or as a percentage of the current price. Additionally, SL can be a percentage of TP, and TP can be a percentage of SL, allowing for interdependent risk-reward ratios.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\TriAngle Runtrend 1 shot V1.01.mq4

topic: Trade Management by Group and Magic Number
description: Trades are managed using a 'Group' parameter and a 'MagicStart' number. The Magic Number for each trade is `MagicStart + (int)Group`. This allows the EA to identify and manage its own trades, and potentially differentiate between different strategies or instances of the EA on the same account.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\TriAngle Runtrend 1 shot V1.01.mq4

description: The EA features a trailing stop mechanism that can be applied to a group of trades. It calculates an average price and average lots for the group. The trailing stop can be set based on fixed pips, dynamic pips (from a function), money amount, or a percentage of the trailing stop itself. It also includes a 'Trailing Start Level' to define when the trailing should begin (e.g., after a certain profit in pips or percentage of the stop size). The trailing stop can be applied to either Stop Loss or Take Profit levels.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\TriAngle Runtrend 1 shot V1.01.mq4

topic: Virtual Stops and Emergency Hard Stops
description: The EA includes a mechanism for 'Virtual Stops' which are managed internally by the EA rather than by the broker. It also has an option for 'Emergency Stops' (hard stops) which can be used as a fallback when virtual stops are enabled, or always. Emergency stops can be set as a multiple of virtual stops or with an additional pip value.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\TriAngle Runtrend 1 shot V1.01.mq4

description: Trades can be set to expire after a specified duration (days, hours, minutes) or at a dynamic time determined by a function. The expiration mode can be 'GTC' (Good Till Canceled) or time-based, providing flexibility for time-sensitive strategies.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\TriAngle Runtrend 1 shot V1.01.mq4

topic: Grid Order Placement with Multiple Levels
description: The EA implements a multi-level grid system with separate Buy Stop, Sell Stop, Buy Limit, and Sell Limit orders. Each order type has configurable parameters: OrdersBuyStop, OrdersSellStop, OrdersBuyLimit, OrdersSellLimit define the count of orders per type. FirstLevelStop/FirstLevelLimit set the initial distance from current price, while StepBuyStop, StepSellStop, StepBuyLimit, StepSellLimit define spacing between successive orders. The system tracks the highest Buy Stop price (OOPBS) and lowest Sell Stop price (OOPSS) to place new orders at progressively worse prices, creating a recovery grid.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\cm manual grid V_2.mq4

topic: Manual Grid Control via UI Panel
description: The EA creates an interactive control panel with buttons and editable fields for real-time parameter adjustment. Users can toggle Buy Stop, Sell Stop, Buy Limit, Sell Limit grid activation independently. Edit fields allow live modification of OrdersCount, FirstLevel, Step, Lot, LotPlus, K_Lot, and profit targets without recompilation. Global variables persist state across ticks, enabling manual override of trading logic.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\cm manual grid V_2.mq4

topic: Portfolio Limits - Maximum Buy and Sell Positions
description: MaxBuy and MaxSell parameters enforce hard limits on total open positions. The system counts all Buy orders (OP_BUY + OP_BUYSTOP + OP_BUYLIMIT) and Sell orders (OP_SELL + OP_SELLSTOP + OP_SELLLIMIT). New orders are only placed if b+bl+bs < MaxBuy or s+sl+ss < MaxSell. This prevents over-leveraging and provides drawdown protection by capping exposure.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\cm manual grid V_2.mq4

topic: Order Closure by Opposite Position Pairing
description: CloseByOrders() function implements hedging closure: it pairs Buy and Sell positions and closes them against each other using OrderCloseBy(). This is useful for grid systems where opposite orders accumulate. The function iterates through all orders, identifies the first Buy and first Sell, closes them together, then repeats. Includes error handling for trade context busy (error 146) and price refresh (error 129).
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\cm manual grid V_2.mq4

description: Three closure modes: CloseProfit B/S closes Buy/Sell positions individually when their profit reaches thresholds; ProfitClose closes all positions when total profit reaches target. Each has an 'auto' button that automatically triggers closure when profit threshold is met. The system displays current profit on buttons and changes color (green/red) based on P&L. TradeOFF parameter disables grid orders after profit closure.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\cm manual grid V_2.mq4

description: When 'Tral Profit' button is activated, the EA tracks the highest profit reached (StopProfit variable). TrailStart defines the profit level to activate trailing, TrailClose defines the drawdown tolerance. Once profit >= TrailStart, StopProfit is set. If profit subsequently drops below StopProfit - TrailClose, all positions are closed. This protects gains by locking in profits with a trailing stop mechanism.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\cm manual grid V_2.mq4

topic: Dynamic Lot Sizing with Coefficient Multiplication
description: Lot sizing uses a formula: Lot (base lot) + LotPlus (increment) * K_Lot (coefficient). For the first order, lots = Lot. For subsequent orders in the grid, lots = PreviousOrderLots * K_Lot + LotPlus. This creates exponential or linear lot increase depending on K_Lot value. Example: Lot=0.1, LotPlus=0.1, K_Lot=1.0 produces 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4 progression.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\cm manual grid V_2.mq4

topic: Magic Number Filtering and Multi-Symbol Support
description: The EA uses Magic parameter (default -1) to filter orders. When Magic = -1, all orders on the symbol are processed regardless of magic number. When Magic has a specific value, only orders with matching magic are managed. This allows multiple EA instances on the same symbol without interference. Symbol() function ensures only current chart symbol orders are affected.
source_file: G:\My Drive\jobot\cm manual grid V_2.mq4
